frameworks.name: real-time fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: voluntary self-regulation of brain activity via closed-loop neural signal feedback

frameworks.name: brain-computer interface
frameworks.one_line: virtual environment serves as BCI to deliver feedback during emotion induction

frameworks.name: support vector machine classifier
frameworks.one_line: multivariate pattern decoding compared against ROI-based feedback signal

core_question: Can rtfMRI neurofeedback combined with VR and music sustain voluntary regulation of complex emotional states?
key_assumptions: neural correlates of complex emotion can be measured in real time
personalized strategies enhance induced emotion intensity
ROI and SVM signals both index the target emotional state
manipulations: feedback signal type (ROI vs SVM classifier) across two days
counterbalanced day order across two experimental sites
key_findings: participants voluntarily enhanced neural activity while experiencing complex emotions
multimodal VR/NFB approach was technically feasible and robust across sites
interpretations: rtfMRI-NFB with VR/music can measure and modulate complex emotional states with ecological validity
limitations_noted: small sample (eight participants) framed as technical proof of concept
